raw.population: [Hypothermia] N: 20 (USA) GA (weeks): 39.5±1.7 WT: 3.5±0.58 [No hypothermia] N: 19 (USA) GA (weeks): 38.7±1.8 WT: 3.5±0.67 Mean PNA in total study population: 13.1
raw.objective: “to identify and quantify the effects of TH and patients’ maturational changes on phenobarbital PK in neonates with HIE, using a population PK model” (TH: therapeutic hypothermia, HIE: hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy)
raw.covariates: Tested: SEX, GA, PNA, birth WT, WT, APGAR score, AST, ALT Included: WT, PNA on CL; WT on Vd
raw.observations: Model seems to predict reasonably well, but assumes that clearance at birth is near 0% of adult value (when already adjusted for weight). This seems hardly acceptable, but validity of this model cannot be determined, as range of PNA in the data is not given. A model using PMA or GA would probably have been more acceptable, and could perhaps also be used for pre-term neonates.
raw.equations: PNAc50=theta3 CL=theta1*((WT/70)**0.75)*(PNA/(PNA+PNAc50))*exp(eta1) Vd=theta2*(WT/70) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1)+eps2 PNA is postnatal age in days
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.672 theta2=64.9 theta3=22.1 omega1=0.175 sigma1=0.197 sigma2=6.12

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.population: [Hypothermia] N: 20 (USA) GA (weeks): 39.5±1.7 WT: 3.5±0.58 [No hypothermia] N: 19 (USA) GA (weeks): 38.7±1.8 WT: 3.5±0.67 Mean PNA in total study population: 13.1
study.objective: “to identify and quantify the effects of TH and patients’ maturational changes on phenobarbital PK in neonates with HIE, using a population PK model” (TH: therapeutic hypothermia, HIE: hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy)

covariates.raw: Tested: SEX, GA, PNA, birth WT, WT, APGAR score, AST, ALT Included: WT, PNA on CL; WT on Vd

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
